inlineContent.text: surfaces the packaged build version for diagnostics.
Full face-recognition functionality requires a real iOS device.
Construct once at app launch (e.g. inside your composition root) and hold a
reference. The SDK is reference-typed; share the instance.
The embedded face-recognition engine is the Luxand FaceSDK.
: a single shared instance is designed to be handed to
session actor and the host’s actor. The engine processes one frame at a
time in practice; this conformance formalises that existing contract so
Swift 6 consumers can share the instance without a data-race diagnostic.

~60 s budget), the walk’s FRR evidence —
Every currently-enrolled identity (name, faceID, centroid count, radii,
pose-0 snapshot PNG), sorted by name. Drives a host “manage faces” list.
Enrol a reference identity directly from a still image (no camera) for
offline / FIDO 1:1 testing: extracts the face template and appends it to
images against an image-sourced reference. Returns
when the image carries no usable face. Persist the result with
recognition still expects a camera enrolment — this path feeds the
Begin a multi-pose (“gallery”) enrollment with caller-supplied
pose targets. The Luxand walk learns each pose’s appearances straight
into the tracker as the named face is held in view — identity is the
tracker’s own confidence-gated decision; there is
embedding vetting or 0.95 radius-floor on this engine. Capture only the
intended person: a bystander who steps into the
frame during the walk can be learned into the gallery.
TrueDepth, on supported devices — into the trust pipeline.
Host-driven “Save now” for the lighting pass: finalise the gallery with
whatever lighting breadth has been gathered, instead of holding on “move
into a new light” until the deadlock backstop. Lets the UI offer an
escape so a user whose room can’t yield more distinct light isn’t
stranded by the breadth-gated stall. Idempotent and safe to call outside
Remove a single enrolled identity, leaving the others intact. Returns
when an identity by that name was removed,
persist afterward so the deletion survives relaunch.

sessions. Hosts poll this each frame to drive per-pose UI (progress

Scope live recognition to a single enrolled identity (“verify on this
person”): when set, the matcher compares only against that face’s gallery,
so the trust gate fires only for them. Pass
enrolled identity (best match wins). A name with no enrolled identity
yields no matches until it’s enrolled or reset.
